text.title: Creating or Updating Claude Code Skills Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Author wraps a 'write me a haiku' prompt as a skill. Auto-triggers on too many keywords; pollutes `/` menu.
antipattern.root-cause: Skill abused as a prompt cache for a one-off task.
antipattern.fix: Use a plain prompt for one-shot tasks. Skills are for reusable, discoverable workflows.

antipattern.symptom: Read-only research skill has full Bash + Write. Bug causes file deletion during 'research'.
Project-specific 'deploy-to-acme-staging' skill committed to faion-network. Cross-project conflicts grow.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated faion-network as a personal skill bag.
antipattern.fix: Project-specific → `.claude/skills/` in project repo + gitignore. faion-network only for shared cross-project skills.

antipattern.symptom: Skill `lint-code` steals every `lint`-mention call from `code-review`. Users frustrated.
antipattern.fix: 10-phrasing test: confirm intended auto-fire + non-overlap with peers.
